summary: Descriptive overview of paternity in substantive law: presumption, genetic testing, disestablishment.
key_points: presumption is a fundamental concept in family law.
Legal standards for paternity are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine paternity in litigation.
The doctrine of paternity has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding paternity requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
